name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 02.02.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 02.02.2026.pdf

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 30, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 676.84 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 96.20 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 660.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 30, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 676.84 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 96.20 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 660.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 23.01.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India .
texts.text:  As on 23.01.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum  temperatures  are likely to  be  below  normal  East  India, West Rajasthan,  Chhattisgarh,  Vidarbha,  North  Karnataka,  Telangana  and  Kerala. TMIN  is likely  to  be  above  normal  in  J&K,  Gujarat,  East  Rajasthan,  Madhya  Pradesh,  MadhyaMaharashtra, South Karnataka and NE India.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum  temperatures  are likely to  be  below  normal  East  India, West Rajasthan,  Chhattisgarh,  Vidarbha,  North  Karnataka,  Telangana  and  Kerala. TMIN  is likely  to  be  above  normal  in  J&K,  Gujarat,  East  Rajasthan,  Madhya  Pradesh,  MadhyaMaharashtra, South Karnataka and NE India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- February 01 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  32%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  87%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  11%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  79%;  and South Peninsula: 8%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- February 01 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  32%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  87%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  11%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  79%;  and South Peninsula: 8%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Under the influence of two Western Disturbance in quick succession, light to moderate scattered/ fairly widespread rainfall/snowfall likely over Western Himalayan region and light to moderate isolated/scattered rainfall over adjoining plains of northwest India and Central India during 01 st  to 3 rd  February. Thereafter, a third western disturbance is likely to affect Western Himalayan region during 05 th  - 07 th  February, 2026.
texts.text:  Under the influence of two Western Disturbance in quick succession, light to moderate scattered/ fairly widespread rainfall/snowfall likely over Western Himalayan region and light to moderate isolated/scattered rainfall over adjoining plains of northwest India and Central India during 01 st  to 3 rd  February. Thereafter, a third western disturbance is likely to affect Western Himalayan region during 05 th  - 07 th  February, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall/snowfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty winds likely  over  Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad  and  Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand during 02nd -03rd February.
texts.text:  Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall/snowfall  with  thunderstorm,  lightning  &  gusty winds likely  over  Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad  and  Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand during 02nd -03rd February.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated to Scattered light rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning with gusty winds likely over Uttar Pradesh and East Rajasthan on 2 nd  February & Madhya Maharashtra during 02 nd  February 2026.
texts.text:  Isolated to Scattered light rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning with gusty winds likely over Uttar Pradesh and East Rajasthan on 2 nd  February & Madhya Maharashtra during 02 nd  February 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be below normal over the country except the Western Himalayan Region and Northwest India, where it is likely  to be  near  normal to  above normal.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be below normal over the country except the Western Himalayan Region and Northwest India, where it is likely  to be  near  normal to  above normal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense  fog  conditions  likely  during  morning/night  hours  at  a  few  places  over  Punjab, Haryana, and Chandigarh till 03 rd  February and at isolated pockets till 5 th  February.
texts.text:  Dense  fog  conditions  likely  during  morning/night  hours  at  a  few  places  over  Punjab, Haryana, and Chandigarh till 03 rd  February and at isolated pockets till 5 th  February.

texts.text: CROP WEATHER WATCH GROUP (CWWG) WEEKLY REPORT

texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 29 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 126.354 billion cubic meter, which is 69%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 107.65 percent of last year's storage and 124.16 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 29 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 126.354 billion cubic meter, which is 69%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 107.65 percent of last year's storage and 124.16 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
